rulings.Case Title: INMA PENA VS. BLACKROCK INSTITUTIONAL TRUST COMPANY, NA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Demurrer To First Amended Complaint (First Cause Of Action -Promissory Fraud And Fifth Cause Of Action--Violation Of Cal. Labor Code 970); Compendium Of Non-California Authorities In Support Of Demurrer To First Amended Complaint;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 22, 2009, line 8, DEFENDANT BARCLAYS GLOBAL INVESTORS, NA Demurrer To First Amended Complaint. Demurrer overuled, ten days leave to answer. Claim for promisory fraud sufficient. Although one-year statute of limitations applies to section 970 claim, it begins to accrue when the employee loses her job, not earlier when she discovers the fraud. Aguilera vs. Pirelli (2000) 223 F.3d 1010, 1018. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH TSANG et al VS. BRUCE M MCCORMACK MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 22, 2009, line 9, DEFENDANT BRUCE MCCORMACK AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to the second cause of action for lack of informed consent and the third cause of action for failure to refer to a specialist. These are duplicative causes of action to the first cause of action for medical negligence and add nothing to the complaint by way of fact or theory. No reasonable probability that defect could be cured by amendment. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action for medical battery to state facts that support the claim. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SYERSS PROPERTIES I,LLP A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. BANC OF AMERICA INVESTMENT SERVICES INC A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition To Compel Arbitration; And 2) Motion To Stay Proceedings; Compendium Of Non California Authroites
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 22, 2009, line 10, DEFENDANTS BANC OF AMERICA INVESTMENT SERVICES, INC., BANK OF AMERICA, N.A., AND, BANK OF AMERICA CORPORATION Notice Of Petition To Compel Arbitration; and, 2) Motion To Stay Proceedings. Motion to compel arbitration granted. Motion to stay proceedings granted. The terms of the application/agreement are clearly and precisely written, and by signing, plaintiff expressly agreed that he received, read, understood and agreed to arbitration, and acknowledged receiving a copy of the agreement which contained a clear and unabiguous arbitration clause. Mr. Kern personally handed a copy of the Customer agreement to plaintiff. The terms of the arbitration clause are binding. FINRA would be the most appropriate current forum to arbitrate disputes because NASD is no longer in existence and FINRA is its successor. No procedural or substantive unconscionability demonstrated. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JOE ELDRIDGE III et al VS. UNITED PARCEL SERVICE INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Eldridge Joe, Iii And William King'S Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction (Per Stipulation & Order Of 09/23/09)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 22, 2009, line 13, Plaintiffs Eldridge Joe, III, and William King's Order to Show Cause re: preliminary Injunction. Motion for preliminary injuction denied. Plaintiffs fail to demonstrate an inadequate remedy at law. Plaintiffs' remedy is to sue for damages in an employment case. This is not a class action and plaintiffs do not have representional standing. Plaintiffs' delay in bringing this action shows that there is no imminent harm. Plaintiffs fail to show a liklihood of prevailing on the merits. Vehicle Code section 22502 (b) allows commercial vehicles to double park when it "is reasonably necessary to accomplish the loading and unloading." It is unclear that plaintiffs' supervisors were telling plaintiffs to break the law. The proposed injunction is overbroad because it would give the drivers authority to refuse to double park in all instances, even when it was indisputably legal. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: LUIS ALJEJO et al VS. JACK O'CONNELL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioners Luis Aljejo And Maria Medina'S Amended Petitioners' Motion For Preliminary Injunction (Continued From 10/20/09 To 10/22/09)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 22, 2009, line 18, Petitioners Luis Aljejo and Maria Medina's Amended Petitions' motion for preliminary injunction. Motion for preliminary injunction denied. Where mandamus is available, the equitable remedy of an injunction is ordinarily improper. Moore vs. Superior Court (1936) 6 Cal.2nd 421. Insufficient showing of imminent or irreparable harm. Defendant is still employing programs other than on-site monitoring to assist LEP students and is not abandoning them. No showing plaintiff likely to prevail on the merits. Insufficient showing that Education Code section 52177(d) (three year on-site monitoring cycle) is still operative in view of sunset provision of EC section 62000.2(c). EC section 62002 specifies the relevant statutes adopted regarding the use of funds shall not be operative except as specified in EC section 62002.5, which does not mention the continuation of on-site montoring. Defendant has broad discretion to establish the process and frequency of on-site reviews. The EEOC does not specifically require on-site monitoring and the evidence shows defendant is still taking steps to protect LEP students while trying to fine-tune the monitoring program. Plaintiff is also attempting to change the status quo by seeking a three year monitoring cycle where there has historically only been a four year cycle. =(302/CWW)
